title: Lucid Motors Names New CEO, Lands More Money from Uber and Saudis
text: Lucid Motors: CEO Transition, Funding, and Uber Partnership Update. CEO Transition. Silvio Napoli, a veteran industrial executive from Schindler Group, has been appointed as Lucid's new CEO after a search lasting over a year following Peter Rawlinson's departure. Marc Winterhoff, the interim COO, will return to his previous role once Napoli fully assumes the position. Napoli's compensation package includes a $1.5 million base salary, $1 million relocation allowance, and stock awards potentially worth tens of millions. Funding Details. Two major funding announcements accompanied the CEO appointment: Uber Investment: An additional $200 million commitment bringing total investment to $500 million, with orders expanding from 20,000 to 35,000 vehicles. Saudi PIF: A $550 million share purchase by the company's majority owner. Uber Partnership Expansion. Uber and autonomous vehicle partner Nuro began testing modified Lucid Gravity SUVs for a luxury robotaxi service launching in San Francisco. The agreement reduces minimum Gravity SUV orders to 10,000 while adding 15,000 units of Lucid's upcoming mid-size vehicle platform.
